claims.statement: Under york_diagnostic_contract@v1 and lane_a_eulerian_comoving_theta_minus_trk, the current live York reduced-order deterministic congruence artifact classifies the NHM2 certified snapshot as nhm2_low_expansion_family under this diagnostic-local contract; cross-lane status is governed by lane readiness gates and must be read from the current artifact outputs.

validityDomain.system: York reduced-order deterministic congruence under lane A
constraints: theta definition remains theta=-trK in ADM sign convention
family verdict requires all proof-pack preconditions to pass
cross-lane comparison is allowed only when both lanes close parity/identity/readiness gates under the same contract

sources.note: Declares the York diagnostic lane, feature set, weights, and robustness policy.
Machine-readable reduced-order snapshot for the current Lane A verdict.
Human-readable proof-pack audit with boundary statement, lane preconditions, and robustness summary.
Drift report for reduced-order artifacts; first run is expected to be inconclusive until a distinct prior artifact exists.
Low-expansion calibration anchor for the Natario control family.
Signed-lobe calibration anchor for the Alcubierre control family.

falsifier.condition: A same-contract rerun changes the family label, drops robustness below the marginal threshold, fails proof-pack preconditions, or yields a supported alternate-lane disagreement.
falsifier.note: This claim must be downgraded to inconclusive if the diagnostic-local preconditions or stability policy stop holding.

claims.notes: This registry entry intentionally caps the claim at diagnostic CL2 and keeps the result inside lane-local reduced-order scope.

claims.statement: Under warp_render_congruence_benchmark_contract@v1 and render_metric_parity_lane_a, the render congruence benchmark currently remains inconclusive due to insufficient displacement events; this claim is restricted to diagnostic-local renderer/observable congruence governance.

constraints: benchmark claim is diagnostic-local and not a feasibility statement
minimum displacement-event threshold must be met for a conclusive parity verdict
observable parity anchors are interpreted only inside the benchmark contract
cross-lane interpretation is unavailable until a supported alternate benchmark lane exists

sources.note: Defines benchmark contract id, lane id, and status mapping for the render parity producer.
Human-readable benchmark audit for render and observable parity lanes.
Drift report for benchmark contract identity and evidence stability.

falsifier.condition: The benchmark must be downgraded if displacement events remain below threshold, if observable/render status diverges under the same contract, or if benchmark artifacts drift under unchanged contract metadata.
falsifier.note: This claim remains diagnostic-local and can validly stay inconclusive until sufficient benchmark evidence accumulates.

claims.notes: This registry entry governs the render congruence benchmark as a diagnostic CL2 producer and explicitly disallows feasibility or frame-identity interpretation.
